name: EZEPTOCORE
description: Algorithmic 8 HP breakbeat sampler. Streams stereo 16-bit 44.1 kHz audio from SD (16 banks x 16 samples), slices and re-sequences it with a Turing-style rungler, and layers 16 live-switchable effects. Eurorack port of the open-source zeptocore.

controls.name: X
controls.description: Main performance knob. Adjusts the parameter of the currently selected effect or, combined with button holds, controls pitch, tempo and bank/sample.
controls.detail: context-sensitive; held with A/B/C for global settings

controls.name: Y
controls.description: Second effect parameter for the active FX preset. Shapes depth or character alongside X.

controls.name: Z
controls.description: Sets probability for the AMEN/BREAK engine and doubles as master volume when held with A.

controls.name: A
controls.description: Hold to access global functions: +X for tempo, +Z for volume, +B tap for manual tempo tap (30-300 BPM).

controls.name: B
controls.description: Hold B then tap a key 1-16 to pick a bank, keep holding and tap another key 1-16 to pick the sample within that bank.

controls.name: C
controls.description: Switches between play modes (jump/manual, loop, retrigger) and into mash mode or bass mode for the 16 keys.

controls.name: D
controls.description: Arms and captures the real-time sequencer so key presses and FX toggles are looped back as a pattern.

controls.name: 16 keys
controls.description: Sixteen numbered keys. In jump mode each key jumps between 16 slices of the current sample, in mash mode they toggle the 16 effects, in bass mode they play a chromatic bass across the keyboard.

inputs.name: Clock In
inputs.description: External clock input. Drives the internal sequencer, slice timing and tempo-locked effects.

outputs.name: Audio Out L
outputs.description: Left channel of the stereo sample and FX output.

outputs.name: Audio Out R
outputs.description: Right channel of the stereo sample and FX output.

outputs.name: Clock Out
outputs.description: Internal clock with on-board divider and multiplier, useful for syncing other modules to the break.

outputs.name: Trigger Out
outputs.description: Analysis-derived trigger mirrored from the sample: kick, snare or hi-hat extraction follows the loop's rhythm and fires gates for the rest of the rack.

behaviors.name: Rungler break order (AMEN)
behaviors.trigger: AMEN parameter (knob X in performance mode)
behaviors.description: AMEN fully counter-clockwise plays slices in their original order. As you turn it up the built-in Turing/rungler sequencer recombines slices, and at full travel it cooks up a brand new slice + FX combination every pass of the loop.

behaviors.name: FX probability (BREAK)
behaviors.description: BREAK controls how often the sixteen effects (and their combinations) are sprinkled onto the sample. Low = clean loop, high = wall-to-wall effect mayhem.

behaviors.name: 16 live-switchable effects
behaviors.description: Effects include saturation, loss, fuzz, bit-crush, time-stretch, delay, comb filter, repeat, tighten, expand, circulate, scratch, lower, reverse and tape-stop. Any of them can be toggled from the 16 keys or automated by BREAK.

behaviors.name: Time-stretch to clock
behaviors.description: Sample playback time-stretches to match the incoming clock (or tapped tempo from 30-300 BPM), so breaks stay in time when the master clock wobbles.

behaviors.name: Stereo SD streaming
behaviors.description: Audio streams directly from the SD card in 16-bit 44.1 kHz stereo, so you can hot-swap between any of 256 samples (16 banks x 16 slots) without loading into RAM.

behaviors.name: Percussion trigger extraction
behaviors.description: The engine analyses incoming slices and fires Trigger Out on kick, snare or hi-hat hits inside the loop, giving you a live drum trigger track from any break.

patchIdeas.name: Evolving amen break
patchIdeas.patch: Load a classic breakbeat into bank 1 · feed a master clock into Clock In · crank AMEN (X) so the rungler re-orders slices · nudge BREAK (Y) to sprinkle FX · stereo out into the mixer for a constantly mutating amen.
patchIdeas.diagram:
EZEPTOCORE:
* X (AMEN): 1 o'clock
* Y (BREAK): 11 o'clock
* Z: noon

Clock:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) c> EZEPTOCORE (Clock In)
- EZEPTOCORE (Audio Out L) -> Mixer (In L)
- EZEPTOCORE (Audio Out R) -> Mixer (In R)
steps: Copy a drum break as break.wav onto the SD card (bank 1, slot 1).
Feed a steady 4/4 clock from your master clock into Clock In.
Hold B, tap key 1 to pick bank, then tap key 1 again for the sample.
Turn X (AMEN) up slowly - you will hear slices start to recombine.
Nudge Y (BREAK) clockwise to taste so FX begin to flicker in.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Audio Out L + R into the mixer
patchIdeas.hear: Familiar amen groove that slowly rearranges itself, with bursts of stretch, reverse and bit-crush riding on top.

patchIdeas.name: Break as drum trigger source
patchIdeas.patch: Clock EZEPTOCORE from the rack clock · loop a breakbeat · take Trigger Out into an envelope/VCA on another drum voice so the extracted kick/snare hits play an external drum along with the break.
patchIdeas.diagram:
EZEPTOCORE:
* X (AMEN): low
* Y (BREAK): low

Clock:
Envelope:
Drum Voice:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) c> EZEPTOCORE (Clock In)
- EZEPTOCORE (Trigger Out) t> Envelope (Trig)
- Envelope (Out) >> Drum Voice (CV)
- EZEPTOCORE (Audio Out L) -> Mixer (In)
- Drum Voice (Out) -> Mixer (In)
steps: Keep AMEN low so the original break order stays intact.
Patch Trigger Out to a percussive envelope's trigger input.
Route that envelope into a drum voice or noise-burst module.
Sum the drum voice and the EZEPTOCORE audio into the mixer.
Adjust Y (BREAK) gently - clean break, extra drum layered on top.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Audio Out L (or L+R) plus external drum voice into mixer
patchIdeas.hear: The break playing normally while a second drum voice plays along, doubling the kick/snare hits extracted from the loop.

patchIdeas.name: Mash-mode effect performance
patchIdeas.patch: Hold C to enter mash mode so the 16 keys become FX toggles · tap keys live while the break loops to throw delay, tape-stop, reverse and bit-crush on and off like a DJ's effect bar.
patchIdeas.diagram:
EZEPTOCORE:
* X (AMEN): 10 o'clock
* Y (BREAK): 9 o'clock
* Z: noon

Clock:
Mixer:

- Clock (Out) c> EZEPTOCORE (Clock In)
- EZEPTOCORE (Audio Out L) -> Mixer (In L)
- EZEPTOCORE (Audio Out R) -> Mixer (In R)
steps: Hold C and tap the key for mash mode (see the colour cue on the keys).
Start the master clock so the sample loops cleanly.
Tap a key to toggle an effect - it stays on until tapped again.
Combine keys for stacked FX (eg reverse + tape-stop).
Use D to record the tap performance back into the sequencer.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Audio Out L + R into the mixer
patchIdeas.hear: A clean break that you are rewriting live - stutters, reverses and tape stops dropping in exactly where you tap.

patchIdeas.name: Clock divider for the rack
patchIdeas.patch: Use EZEPTOCORE's Clock Out with its divider to feed slower clocks to envelopes and sequencers while the break plays at full tempo · the whole rack locks to the sample's downbeat.
patchIdeas.diagram:
EZEPTOCORE:
* X (AMEN): 10 o'clock
* Y (BREAK): 9 o'clock

Master Clock:
Sequencer:
Envelope:
Mixer:

- Master Clock (Out) c> EZEPTOCORE (Clock In)
- EZEPTOCORE (Clock Out) c> Sequencer (Clock In)
- EZEPTOCORE (Clock Out) c> Envelope (Trig)
- EZEPTOCORE (Audio Out L) -> Mixer (In L)
- EZEPTOCORE (Audio Out R) -> Mixer (In R)
steps: Patch your master clock into Clock In.
Set Clock Out to a useful division (eg /4) in the settings menu.
Send Clock Out into a sequencer's clock input.
Also patch Clock Out to an envelope trigger for bass plucks.
Keep the stereo audio going to the mixer - everything now runs on the break's grid.
patchIdeas.signalOut: Audio Out L + R to mixer; Clock Out to the rest of the rack
patchIdeas.hear: A tight break with sequenced bass and envelope accents all breathing on the same subdivision.
